title: fraxinus sieboldiana f. quadrijuga
intro: Fraxinus sieboldiana f. quadrijuga, also known as the Siebold's ash tree, is a deciduous tree species in the genus Fraxinus, characterized by its compound leaves and quadrifoliate leaflets. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, attributed to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Fraxinus sieboldiana f. quadrijuga include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and protection against infections. Fraxinus sieboldiana f. quadrijuga can be used in various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
